AGENTS.md — MPCurver Package

Project Overview

MPCurver (formerly smoothEMr) implements the MPCurve statistical model for trajectory/pseudotime inference in high-dimensional data (e.g. single-cell RNA-seq). The package now has two fitting families:

  1. cavi — the recommended/default variational pipeline, with an explicit Gaussian posterior over feature trajectories.
  2. csmooth_em / smooth_em — legacy MAP/EM-style algorithms retained for benchmarking, backward compatibility, and method comparison.

All user-facing workflows should be framed around fit_mpcurve() / do_mpcurve(), which default to the cavi backend.

Package goals: 1. Performance — vectorize hot loops, enforce sparse matrix usage, add parallelism 2. API usability — cleaner pipe-friendly interface, better defaults, unified naming 3. Documentation & tests — testthat coverage, parameter-choice vignette

Current Status Snapshot

  • Recommended single-ordering path: fit_mpcurve(..., algorithm = "cavi")
  • Recommended dual-ordering path: soft_two_trajectory_cavi(), forward_two_ordering_partition_cavi(), backward_two_ordering_partition_cavi()
  • Legacy benchmark path: csmooth_em / smooth_em plus the older *_csmooth() and soft_two_trajectory_EM() functions
  • Current theory/docs entry points: vignettes/mpcurve_intro.Rmd, vignettes/partition.Rmd, vignettes/cavi_math_details.Rmd, vignettes/cavi_vs_csmooth_math.Rmd, vignettes/elife_fitness_analysis.Rmd
  • Historical note: vignettes/Intro.Rmd is retained only as archival / pre-cavi context and should not be treated as the current package introduction.

Architecture: Three-Layer Class Hierarchy

There are four S3 classes. Do NOT mix them up:

Class File Description
cavi 09_cavi.R Recommended variational MPCurve fitter with posterior mean/covariance for trajectories
smooth_em 03_SmoothEM.R Original: shared covariance, single global precision Q
csmooth_em 06_cSmoothEM.R Enhanced: coordinate-specific lambda_j, diagonal covariance
mpcurve 07_mpcurve.R User-facing unified wrapper around cavi, csmooth_em, or smooth_em

The mpcurve class is the recommended user interface. It wraps a raw cavi, csmooth_em, or smooth_em fit, normalises the fields into a consistent layout, and exposes fit_mpcurve() / do_mpcurve() as the primary entry points.

The cavi class is the main algorithm under active development. The csmooth_em class is kept as a legacy/benchmark path. The smooth_em class is kept for backward compatibility.

Key fields of a cavi object:

$params        # list(pi, sigma2) — current parameter block
  $pi          # length-K mixing proportions
  $sigma2      # length-d feature-specific variances; NULL when known measurement SD is supplied via S
$measurement_sd # optional known measurement SD specification
                # length-d vector (feature-shared) or n×d matrix (observation-level)
$posterior     # list(mean, cov, diag)
  $mean        # d×K posterior mean matrix for feature trajectories
  $cov         # list of length d; each entry is a K×K covariance matrix S_j
  $diag        # d×K matrix of posterior marginal variances diag(S_j)
$gamma         # n×K responsibility matrix q(C)
$data          # n×d data matrix
$Q_K           # K×K RW precision with lambda = 1
$lambda_vec    # length-d feature-specific smoothness parameters
$elbo_trace    # numeric vector — variational ELBO
$loglik_trace  # numeric vector — plug-in observed log-likelihood diagnostic
$iter          # integer — total CAVI sweeps run
$control       # list of control parameters (rw_q, bounds, method, noise_model, etc.)

Key fields of a csmooth_em object:

$params        # list(pi, mu, sigma2) — mixture parameters
  $pi          # length-K mixing proportions
  $mu          # list of K vectors, each length d (means per component)
  $sigma2      # length-d (homoskedastic) or d×K matrix (heteroskedastic)
$gamma         # n×K responsibility matrix (E-step output)
$data          # n×d data matrix
$Q_K           # K×K base precision (RW prior along components; built with lambda=1)
$lambda_vec    # length-d per-coordinate penalty strengths
$elbo_trace    # numeric vector — penalised ELBO per iteration
$loglik_trace  # numeric vector — observed log-likelihood per iteration
$lambda_trace  # list — lambda_vec snapshots per iteration
$ml_trace      # numeric vector — collapsed marginal likelihood per iteration
$iter          # integer — total EM iterations run
$control       # list of control parameters (modelName, rw_q, etc.)

Key fields of an mpcurve object:

$params        # list(pi, mu, sigma2) — unified format
  $pi          # length-K mixing proportions
  $mu          # d×K matrix (one column per component; unified from list)
  $sigma2      # length-d vector (homoskedastic), d×K matrix (heteroskedastic), or NULL for known-noise CAVI fits
$measurement_sd # optional known measurement SD specification carried from the raw cavi fit
$gamma         # n×K responsibility matrix
$elbo_trace    # numeric vector
$loglik_trace  # numeric vector
$lambda_trace  # list
$iter          # integer
$K, $n, $d    # integers — dimensions
$algorithm     # "cavi", "csmooth_em", or "smooth_em"
$modelName     # covariance structure string
$fit           # the underlying cavi / csmooth_em / smooth_em object

File Map

R/
  00_utils.R           # logsumexp, generalized_logdet, small helpers
  01_initialization.R  # All ordering/init methods: PCA, tSNE, Fiedler, isomap, pcurve
                       # Also: parallel_initial_csmoothEM(), parallel_initial()
  02_prior.R           # make_random_walk_precision*(), make_lattice_rwq_precision*()
                       # These return sparse Matrix objects (Matrix package)
  03_SmoothEM.R        # smooth_em class: EM_algorithm(), ESTEP/MSTEP, do_smoothEM()
  04_methods.R         # S3 methods: print/summary/plot for smooth_em and csmooth_em
  05_kriging.R         # kriging_from_precision(), krige_mu_list_to_full_grid()
  06_cSmoothEM.R       # csmooth_em class: as_csmooth_em(), ESTEP_csmooth(), MSTEP_csmooth(),
                       # do_csmoothEM(), do_csmoothEM_ml_collapsed(), initialize_csmoothEM()
  07_mpcurve.R         # mpcurve class: as_mpcurve(), fit_mpcurve(), do_mpcurve()
                       # Unified user-facing API wrapping cavi / smooth_em / csmooth_em
  07_partition.R       # Legacy dual-trajectory csmooth helpers
                       # score_feature_given_Gamma(),
                       # partition_features_twofits(), greedy_backward_filter_csmooth()
  08_SoftSmoothEM.R    # Legacy soft-partition EM with annealing:
                       # soft_two_trajectory_EM(), ESTEP_csmooth_weighted()
  09_cavi.R            # Recommended single-ordering variational pipeline
  10_partition_cavi.R  # Recommended dual-ordering CAVI partition methods:
                       # soft_two_trajectory_cavi(), forward/backward_*_cavi()
  benchmarking_curves.R  # simulate_* functions for testing/benchmarking
  testing.R            # STUB — almost empty, to be replaced by testthat/
log/
  *.md                 # Agent-authored update logs for substantial package changes
plan/
  *.md                 # Implementation plans and scoped work specs

Naming Conventions

  • Functions: snake_case. Internal helpers prefixed with . (e.g. .log_dens_mat_fast)
  • Classes: snake_case (e.g. smooth_em, csmooth_em, mpcurve)
  • S3 generics: follow base R style (print.mpcurve, plot.csmooth_em)
  • %||% null-coalescing operator is defined locally in multiple files — this is intentional (avoid package-level state), do NOT consolidate into 00_utils.R unless refactoring all files

Critical Implementation Details

1. Sparse Matrix Contract

Q_K (the RW precision) is built as a sparse Matrix:: object in 02_prior.R. In as_csmooth_em(), it is immediately coerced to dense (as.matrix(Q_K)) because K is small and dense Cholesky/solve is faster at that scale. This is intentional for the K-dimension precision; do NOT apply this pattern to n×n or d×d matrices.

2. The relative_lambda Scaling

When relative_lambda = TRUE, the prior for coordinate j is scaled by 1/sigma_j^2. This makes the effective smoothness scale-invariant across features. - For homoskedastic: uses the per-coordinate variance sigma2[j] - For heteroskedastic: uses the mixture-weighted average sum_k pi_k * sigma2[j,k] This logic lives in MSTEP_csmooth() in 06_cSmoothEM.R.

This is a legacy csmooth_em feature. The current cavi implementation does not use relative_lambda; it works directly with feature-specific lambda_j.

3. Recommended cavi Update Logic

  • cavi() in 09_cavi.R is the primary implementation.
  • It uses a mean-field variational family over:
    • q(C) — cell positions / responsibilities ($gamma)
    • q(U) — Gaussian posterior over feature trajectories ($posterior)
  • The E-step analogue must include the uncertainty correction from diag(S_j).
  • The quantity that should be monotone for this path is $elbo_trace.
  • $loglik_trace on cavi objects is only a plug-in observed log-likelihood diagnostic. It is not the marginal log-likelihood and not the monotonicity certificate.

4. Legacy E-step / M-step Split

  • ESTEP_csmooth() → updates $gamma (responsibilities), lives in 06_cSmoothEM.R
  • MSTEP_csmooth() → updates $params (pi, mu, sigma2) and $lambda_vec
  • ESTEP_csmooth_weighted() in 08_SoftSmoothEM.R is a monkey-patched version used only inside soft_two_trajectory_EM(). It temporarily overwrites the namespace binding. Do not call it directly outside that context.

5. Convergence Tracking

For the cavi path: - $elbo_trace is the primary objective and should be non-decreasing. - $loglik_trace is a plug-in diagnostic, not the monotonicity certificate.

For the legacy csmooth_em path: do_csmoothEM() appends to $elbo_trace, $loglik_trace, $lambda_trace, and $ml_trace on the underlying csmooth_em object. The ELBO should be non-decreasing; ml_trace (collapsed marginal) is a tighter bound and is the primary objective when adaptive = "ml". The as_mpcurve() converter copies all four traces into the mpcurve wrapper.

6. Lambda Estimation

lambda_vec can be fixed or estimated. When estimated, .estimate_lambda_star() uses a profile likelihood closed form: lambda* = rank(Q) / (mu^T Q mu). This is called inside MSTEP_csmooth() when estimate_lambda = TRUE.

For cavi, each feature-specific lambda_j is updated from the posterior second moment m_j^T Q_K m_j + tr(Q_K S_j) rather than a MAP plug-in estimate.

7. mpcurve as Unified Interface

fit_mpcurve() is the recommended entry point for users. It defaults to algorithm = "cavi", but still supports csmooth_em and smooth_em for legacy comparisons. It returns an mpcurve object. do_mpcurve() continues EM on an existing mpcurve object. as_mpcurve() converts a raw cavi, csmooth_em, or smooth_em fit to mpcurve. The underlying raw fit is always accessible via $fit.

8. Initialization Notes

  • The current cavi() default discretization is "quantile".
  • In harder or model-mismatched examples, "equal" can be a more revealing benchmark, so several diagnostics explicitly compare the two.
  • fiedler_ordering() now auto-increases its default kNN neighbor count when the default graph is disconnected. If the user explicitly supplies k, the function preserves that fixed choice instead of silently changing it.

9. Dual-Ordering Caveat

  • The new cavi partition path has a cleaner objective story than the legacy csmooth_em soft partition path, and its recorded objective_history is the quantity to inspect when checking monotonicity.
  • Empirically, partitioning is often easier than trajectory recovery. In particular, non-monotone feature sets can still be highly initialization-sensitive even when the soft partition objective is well behaved.
  • When debugging dual-ordering behavior, first check whether the same issue is already present in a single-ordering cavi() fit on the true feature subset.

Performance Targets (Enhancement Goals)

Hot Paths to Optimize (in priority order)

  1. cavi() posterior updates (09_cavi.R)
    • Main bottleneck: repeated feature-wise Cholesky solves for S_j / m_j
    • Correctness comes first; do not drop the uncertainty terms just to save time
    • Future target: avoid storing full S_j when only diag(S_j) / tr(Q_K S_j) are needed
  2. ESTEP_csmooth() inner loop (06_cSmoothEM.R)
    • Legacy but still useful for benchmark speed comparisons
    • Currently: for (k in seq_len(K)) over components
    • Target: vectorize using matrix ops; X is n×d, Mu is d×K
  3. Dense matrix coercion (scattered across files)
    • Search for as.matrix() calls on large matrices (not Q_K — that one is intentional)
    • Key culprit: generalized_logdet() in 00_utils.R if applied to large matrices
  4. parallel_initial_csmoothEM() parallelism (01_initialization.R)
    • Already uses parallel::mclapply — on Windows this falls back to serial
    • Consider parallel::parLapply for cross-platform support
  5. Cache invSigma and logdet inside params
    • Already done for smooth_em (.log_dens_mat_fast)
    • Not consistently done for csmooth_em — add caching layer

Remaining API Improvements

  1. Consistent parameter names: rw_q vs q vs order — standardize to rw_q
  2. Legacy marking: documentation should clearly label csmooth_em / smooth_em as benchmark/backward-compatibility paths, not the recommended starting point

Testing Strategy

Use testthat. Test files go in tests/testthat/.

Priority test cases:

  1. test-prior.R — precision matrices are PSD, sparse, correct rank deficiency
  2. test-estep.R — responsibilities sum to 1 row-wise, non-negative
  3. test-mstep.R — pi sums to 1, sigma2 positive
  4. test-elbo.R — ELBO non-decreasing over iterations (use small simulated data)
  5. test-cavi.R — cavi() runs without error and ELBO is monotone on toy data
  6. test-csmooth.R — do_csmoothEM() runs without error on Swiss roll data
  7. test-api.R — fit_mpcurve() produces a valid mpcurve object
  8. test-partition-cavi.R — recommended dual-ordering partition functions run and maintain monotone objective traces where claimed

Test data:

Use simulate_swiss_roll_1d_2d(n=100, seed=1) for fast tests (already in package). Use simulate_spiral2d(n=200, seed=1) for 2D embedding tests.

Dependencies

Current imports (DESCRIPTION): - mclust — used for Mclust initialization and model selection

Also used centrally now: - Matrix - parallel - matrixStats

Style & Code Quality

  • Use base R where possible; avoid tidyverse in core algorithm files
  • magrittr pipe (%>%) is NOT used; native pipe (|>) is fine (R >= 4.1)
  • Roxygen2 for all exported functions; @keywords internal for helpers
  • No library() or require() calls inside package functions
  • options(warn = -1) is never used; warnings should propagate

Agent Update Logs

For any substantial package update, the agent should create a new Markdown log file in log/ so future agents can quickly review what changed.

Treat a change as substantial if it materially affects at least one of: - model behavior or assumptions - public API or object structure - partition / initialization behavior - testing strategy or major new test coverage - documentation needed to understand a new package capability

Log file convention

  • Create one log file per major update
  • Use this filename pattern: log/YYYY-MM-DD-agent-update-title.md
  • Example: log/2026-04-05-codex-known-noise-cavi-support.md

Required log fields

Each log file should clearly include: 1. Agent: e.g. codex or claude 2. Update title: short slug-like title, e.g. known-noise-cavi-support 3. Update date: the date of the update, e.g. 2026-04-05 4. Key updates: concise bullets describing the main implementation changes

Recommended extra fields

When helpful, also include: - key files touched - tests added or run - follow-up notes / caveats for future agents

The goal is not a full changelog. Keep each log file short and high-signal so another agent can skim log/ and quickly recover the package’s recent history.

What NOT to Change

  • Do NOT rename cavi ↔︎ smooth_em ↔︎ csmooth_em ↔︎ mpcurve — would break downstream user code
  • Do NOT change the $elbo_trace / $ml_trace field names on csmooth_em objects
  • Do NOT change the meaning of $elbo_trace on cavi objects; that is the monotone variational objective we use for debugging and guarantees
  • Do NOT remove benchmarking_curves.R simulate functions — used in vignettes
  • The monkey-patch pattern in 08_SoftSmoothEM.R is legacy and still intentional — leave the mechanism, but improvements should preferentially happen in the new cavi-based partition path first

On this page
